rationale: The page provides specific historical and cultural details about the Maitreyavyakarana, a 10th-century Buddhist manuscript. It covers its origin, script, and religious significance, making it suitable for generating questions on Indian art and culture.
cleanedText:
The Maitreyavyakarana (Successfully inscribed in 2017):
* A 10th-century Buddhist manuscript - It prophesies the future arrival of Maitreya Buddha
* Maitreyayvarakarana is a very short text, written on palm leaf in the mixed character of Kutila and Ranjana script, a manuscript of the Pala period (at the time of Gopaladeva, 57th regna; year, approx. 10th century A.D.)
* As per Mahayanic conception Vyakarana signifies a special type of Avadana-Literature relating to future Buddhahood of a being or deity or a chief disciple in a particular world.
